deep_dive.title: Diffusion Language Model for Recommendation
deep_dive.abstract: Large language model (LLM)-empowered recommender systems have emerged as a promising paradigm for generative recommendation, leveraging their strong semantic reasoning and generative capacity to model complex, diverse user preferences. However, most existing approaches rely on an autoregressive paradigm that is suboptimal for recommendation. The next-token objective emphasizes sequential order rather than the structural inter-item dependencies underlying user preferences. In addition, prefix-constrained generation restricts bidirectional context and commits to left-to-right decoding, causing early errors to accumulate without correction. Inspired by the success of diffusion language models, we propose \textbf{DLMRec}, a discrete diffusion language model tailored for recommendation that offers a compelling alternative to autoregressive generation. Specifically, DLMRec introduces three key components to bridge diffusion language modeling with recommendation. First, a collaborative-aware stochastic tokenizer encodes multi-hop collaborative signals into expressive discrete tokens compatible with diffusion modeling. Second, a curriculum-driven training strategy aligns the denoising process with preference recovery through progressive item- and token-level learning. Third, a stability-aware voting mechanism aggregates iterative predictions to improve generation consistency and robustness.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: diffusion language model tailored for recommendation a novel LLM-enhanced approach
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向推荐的扩散语言模型一种新颖的LLM增强方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向推荐的扩散语言模型一种新颖的LLM增强方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向推荐的扩散语言模型一种新颖的LLM增强方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: DLMRec proposes a discrete diffusion language model for recommendation overcoming autoregressive limitations by modeling inter-item dependencies bidirectionally. It achieves superior performance in generative recommendation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对LLM增强推荐中自回归范式的局限性（强调顺序而非项间依赖、双侧上下文受限、早期错误累积）提出DLMRec离散扩散语言模型。DLMRec通过扩散过程双向建模物品依赖避免了自回归的缺陷在生成式推荐任务上取得更优效果为LLM增强推荐开辟了新途径。

deep_dive.title: Cardinality-Decomposed Loss: Matching Training Objectives to Relation Structure in Heterogeneous Recommendation Graphs
deep_dive.abstract: Graph Neural Networks trained on heterogenous bipartite graphs form a common basis in recommendation systems. These graphs often express relations that vary in cardinality, for example, user-item preferences are one-to-many and user-attribute features are one-to-one. Traditionally, a unique loss function is applied for all of the network components which is often Bayesian Personalized Ranking (BPR). While BPR works well for the recommendation task, we find that it causes attribute embeddings to collapse to near-random geometry -- a silent failure that leaves standard ranking metrics largely unaffected and therefore invisible to conventional evaluation. This in turn pollutes user node embeddings, which are shaped by both edge types simultaneously, hurting downstream tasks like personalization, segmentation, etc. Here we propose a Cardinality-Decomposed Loss (CDL) that combines both Cross Entropy (CE) and BPR to enable the model to collectively optimize for relations across cardinalities. We confirm this CE-BPR conflict by showing the two losses compete in the shared encoder's parameter space. We evaluate CDL on five datasets spanning two structural configurations -- one-to-one attributes on user nodes (MovieLens-1M, Last.fm-360K, PayPal Audience Factory, BookCrossing) and on item nodes (Yelp) -- and find that CDL consistently improves discriminability in attribute embeddings. We also show that ranking (NDCG) improves when attributes carry meaningful preference signal, but conflicts with it when the correlation is weak. We use a lambda parameter to navigate this trade-off, and a lambda-sweep reveals that dataset behavior is governed by two graph properties -- semantic alignment and topology leakage. Semantic alignment measures whether the attribute predicts preferences, while topology leakage measures whether the graph's connectivity already encodes it.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: GNN-based recommendation with heterogeneous graphs, introduces cardinality-decomposed loss
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于GNN的异构推荐图方法，提出基数分解损失
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于GNN的异构推荐图方法，提出基数分解损失
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于GNN的异构推荐图方法，提出基数分解损失
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Cardinality-Decomposed Loss addresses attribute embedding collapse in GNN-based recommendation by matching loss functions to relation cardinality.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文发现GNN推荐系统中统一使用BPR损失会导致属性嵌入坍缩至随机几何，虽然不影响排序指标但污染用户嵌入。提出基数分解损失，根据关系基数（一对一、一对多）匹配不同损失函数。实验表明该方法恢复属性嵌入结构并提升推荐鲁棒性，是GNN推荐的重要改进。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文发现GNN推荐系统中统一使用BPR损失会导致属性嵌入坍缩至随机几何，虽然不影响排序指标但污染用户嵌入。提出基数分解损失，根据关系基数（一对一、一对多）匹配不同损失函数。实验表明该方法恢复属性嵌入结构并提升推荐鲁棒性，是GNN推荐的重要改进。

deep_dive.title: Bridging the Structural Gap: Adapting Autoregressive Generation for Recommendation
deep_dive.abstract: Generative Recommendation (GR) has emerged as a new paradigm for sequential recommendation, in which a representative line of work encodes items into hierarchical semantic IDs via residual quantization and predicts the IDs token by token. However, this generative formulation still exhibits structural gaps with respect to the recommendation task: flattening multi-token IDs into a single sequence destroys item-level structure, and the inconsistency between training and inference over a hierarchical codebook gives rise to semantic drift. To bridge these two gaps, we propose BARGE, which employs Item Context-Aware Attention (ICA) to restore item-level structure during encoding, and Hierarchical Path Reranking (HPR) together with Dual-Path Decoding (DPD) to suppress semantic drift from two complementary angles during decoding. Extensive experiments and analytical studies on public benchmarks and a large-scale offline test demonstrate that BARGE achieves superior recommendation performance. An online A/B test on a Tencent platform yields improvements of 0.60% in click-through rate, 1.34% in click unique visitors, and 1.70% in total reading time, confirming the practical value of BARGE in industrial-scale recommendation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: BARGE framework for LLM-enhanced generative recommendation using autoregressive decoding and context-aware attention
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出BARGE框架，利用自回归解码和上下文感知注意力实现LLM增强的生成式推荐
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出BARGE框架，利用自回归解码和上下文感知注意力实现LLM增强的生成式推荐
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出BARGE框架，利用自回归解码和上下文感知注意力实现LLM增强的生成式推荐
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: BARGE bridges structural gaps in generative recommendation by restoring item-level structure with Item Context-Aware Attention and reducing semantic drift via Hierarchical Path Reranking, improving recommendation quality.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 生成式推荐面临多token ID序列破坏物品结构及训练推理不一致导致语义漂移的问题。本文提出BARGE，利用物品上下文感知注意力恢复结构，并采用层级路径重排序与双路径解码减少漂移。实验结果表明BARGE显著提升生成式推荐质量，为LLM增强推荐提供新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 生成式推荐面临多token ID序列破坏物品结构及训练推理不一致导致语义漂移的问题。本文提出BARGE，利用物品上下文感知注意力恢复结构，并采用层级路径重排序与双路径解码减少漂移。实验结果表明BARGE显著提升生成式推荐质量，为LLM增强推荐提供新思路。

deep_dive.title: Can Generative Recommendation Reach Cold Items? A Temporal Perspective on Semantic-ID Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Semantic-ID-based generative recommendation represents items as sequences of shared semantic tokens, enabling token recombination beyond isolated item IDs. However, closed-world recombination does not necessarily imply temporal open-token cold-start induction, where new items enter the item catalog with unseen atomic tokens or weakly supported SID paths. In this work, we revisit SID-based generative recommendation under an absolute-time temporal protocol that separates seen and unseen targets and diagnoses the cold item reachability at the token level. Through seen/unseen-hit analysis, coldness taxonomy, and oracle-prefix probing, we show that current SID-based models can occasionally reach future items supported by observed tokens and prefixes, but struggle with unseen atomic tokens and unsupported SID paths. We further explain this boundary by interpreting SID generation as hierarchical semantic bucketing: early tokens select coarse semantic regions, while later tokens refine item-specific paths. These findings show that SID generation is compositional but not fully open-ended, and suggest future directions in more independent SID spaces, scoring-based interfaces, and dynamic textual context.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: investigates cold-start reachability in generative recommendation using temporal protocol
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用时间协议研究生成式推荐中的冷启动可达性
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用时间协议研究生成式推荐中的冷启动可达性
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用时间协议研究生成式推荐中的冷启动可达性
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper examines whether generative recommendation can reach cold items from a temporal perspective on semantic-ID generation revealing limitations and providing insights for cold-start.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文从时间视角重新审视语义ID生成式推荐通过绝对时间协议分离已见和未见目标在令牌层面诊断冷物品可达性。研究发现当前模型对具有未见过原子令牌的新物品覆盖能力有限揭示了冷启动问题的深层原因并为设计更鲁棒的生成式推荐系统提供了洞察。

deep_dive.title: Evaluating covariate balance for long time horizon Markov decision processes
deep_dive.abstract: This article explores the application of covariate balance diagnostics for detecting the presence of hidden confounding/model miss-specification in studies applying offline reinforcement learning (RL) to deriving optimal treatment recommendations. The results demonstrate that, either there is a high risk of bias within existing offline RL studies for treatment recommendations or, existing covariate balance metrics are not sufficient to assess such studies. Regardless, existing offline RL studies cannot be concluded as being statistically robust. The conclusions propose future research directions for obtaining more methodologically robust applications of offline RL to treatment recommendation problems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Offline RL for deriving optimal treatment recommendations, directly applicable to RL-based recommendation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 离线强化学习用于推导最优治疗建议，直接适用于基于RL的推荐
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 离线强化学习用于推导最优治疗建议，直接适用于基于RL的推荐
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 离线强化学习用于推导最优治疗建议，直接适用于基于RL的推荐
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Evaluates covariate balance diagnostics for offline RL in treatment recommendation, highlighting risk of bias and need for robust methods in RL-based recommendation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文评估了离线强化学习在治疗推荐中的应用，指出现有研究存在高偏倚风险，协变量平衡诊断不足以检测模型误设。通过实验揭示多数离线RL研究统计上不稳健，并提出了改进方向。对于基于RL的推荐系统，本文的方法论分析具有重要警示意义。

quick_skim.title: TSGR: Taobao Search Generative Retrieval
quick_skim.abstract: Generative retrieval (GR) has demonstrated strong promise for industrial e-commerce search by training a single autoregressive model to directly generate the Semantic IDs (SIDs) of target items. However, existing GR systems are primarily optimized for semantic matching and remain insensitive to item business value: SID construction is value-unaware, and candidates are ranked without access to item side-info. Consequently, high-value items are often missed or deprioritized at the retrieval stage, limiting downstream business impact. This limitation is particularly critical in industrial settings such as Taobao Search, where business objectives are central to system design. To address this, we propose $\textbf{T}$aobao $\textbf{S}$earch $\textbf{G}$enerative $\textbf{R}$etrieval ($\textbf{TSGR}$), a unified generative retrieval framework that incorporates value awareness into both item representation and candidate ranking. 1) For item representation, TSGR introduces $\textbf{Query-aware Parallel SID (QP-SID)}$, which encodes query-conditioned value orderings into the SID construction by building parallel codebooks derived from query-item statistics, so that higher-value and query-relevant items are assigned better token indices. 2) For candidate ranking, we introduce a $\textbf{Value-aware Ranking Module (VRM)}$ that is built upon and jointly optimized with the GR, enabling a single model to seamlessly serve as both retriever and pre-ranker without a dedicated pre-ranking stage. A progressive training pipeline further aligns the model with semantic relevance, user preferences, and business objectives. Offline experiments show that TSGR achieves an 9.16% improvement in HR@1000, and online A/B tests further validate its effectiveness, yielding gains of +0.43% in IPV, +1.12% in Transaction Count, and +1.64% in GMV.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: generative retrieval for e-commerce search, applicable to recommendation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 生成式检索方法可迁移至推荐系统中的物品检索
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 生成式检索方法可迁移至推荐系统中的物品检索
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 生成式检索方法可迁移至推荐系统中的物品检索
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TSGR improves generative retrieval by incorporating business value awareness for Taobao Search, relevant to LLM-enhanced recommendation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出TSGR方法，针对生成式检索忽略物品商业价值的问题，在淘宝搜索场景下融入价值感知。通过重构语义ID和排序方式，提升了高价值物品的检索优先级。方法可推广至推荐系统的生成式召回。

quick_skim.title: Controllable and Content-Based Recommendations
quick_skim.abstract: Traditional recommendation systems rely on latent (dense) representations, making them difficult to interpret and control. We propose the Controllable and Content-Based Recommendations (CCBR) framework, which builds its recommendations from textual user profile representations. CCBR plugs into collaborative filtering models and introduces controllability via text bottlenecks. We show that CCBR enables text-based and multimodal interventions, allowing users to steer the model towards the directions they prefer. Different from existing controllable recommendation systems, CCBR infers the text summaries directly from item contents (images, audio or video). Across image-, audio-, and video-based datasets, we demonstrate that the proposed framework obtains competitive model performance with standard (latent-representation) models while providing controllable model summaries via text. The model also outperforms TEARS, a recent baseline for controllable recommendation systems. Through systematic interventions, we demonstrate the efficacy of the user steering mechanism.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于内容的可控推荐框架，与推荐系统主题相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于内容的可控推荐框架，与推荐系统主题相关

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CCBR enables controllable and interpretable recommendations via text bottlenecks inferred from item content, competitive with collaborative filtering.

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 推荐系统相关论文检索，与旅游推荐相关, especially work that connects or combines: GNN-based Recommendation; Intent-aware Recommendation; Cold-start Problem; Reinforcement Learning for Recommendation; Session-based Recommendation; LLM-enhanced Recommendation; Deep reinforcement learning for interactive and conversational recommendation; Graph neural networks for session based recommendation; Solving cold start problem in recommender systems using large language models and knowledge graphs; A survey on large language models for recommender systems.

quick_skim.title: One More Turn, Less Regret: A Regret-Based Multi-Turn Benchmark for LLMs' Clarification Policies
quick_skim.abstract: Ambiguous user requests make clarification a sequential decision problem for conversational LLM assistants: they must decide whether to ask, what to ask, when to stop, and when to answer. We introduce RegretBench, a multi-turn benchmark that evaluates clarification as policy behavior rather than isolated question quality. RegretBench provides a hidden-intent formulation of ambiguity, supports free-form interaction grounded in semantic-state tracking, and introduces a regret-based objective that measures how much value a model loses relative to a reference clarification policy. Experiments on open-domain QA and product recommendation scenarios show that final success alone is insufficient, as models with similar accuracy can differ substantially in efficiency, robustness to user behaviors, and stopping decisions. By jointly measuring intent resolution, interaction cost, ineffective clarification, and regret, RegretBench reveals whether models clarify usefully and efficiently. Our results show that effective clarification requires more than plausible questions: models must ask the right question at the right time and stop once the user's intended meaning is clear.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Regret-based benchmark for LLM clarification policies in multi-turn conversational recommendation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于遗憾的多轮LLM澄清策略基准，应用于产品推荐场景
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于遗憾的多轮LLM澄清策略基准，应用于产品推荐场景
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于遗憾的多轮LLM澄清策略基准，应用于产品推荐场景
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces RegretBench, a multi-turn benchmark evaluating LLM clarification policies via regret-based objectives, tested on open-domain QA and product recommendation, showing that final success alone is insufficient.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM在多轮对话中的澄清策略评估不足问题，提出RegretBench基准。该基准基于隐藏意图建模和遗憾目标，衡量模型相对于最优策略的价值损失。在开放域问答和产品推荐场景中实验表明，仅以最终成功率作为指标存在误导性。为评估对话推荐系统提供了更全面的视角。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Deep reinforcement learning for interactive and conversational recommendation
